data.title: Hollywood tensions rise as Netflix targets Warner Bros.
data.description:
The entertainment capital shifted from mourning to fury when Netflix announced its $83 billion acquisition of Warner Bros. studio and HBO Max. What started as heartbreak over Warner Bros. Discovery’s October sale announcement has erupted into full-scale resistance against what many view as streaming’s most dangerous power grab yet.

Back in October, when the studio first hung its for-sale sign, Hollywood responded with grief. Megaproducer Larry Gordon compared it to losing a family member. Screenwriters filled WhatsApp chats with words like heartbreaking and tragic. But now that Netflix has emerged as the buyer, sentiment has curdled into something sharper.


Industry heavyweights sound alarm

Jane Fonda launched a blistering attack in a letter to an entertainment trade publication, warning the merger represents an alarming escalation in a consolidation crisis that threatens the entire entertainment industry, the public it serves and potentially even First Amendment protections. Her stark assessment captures growing fears that streaming monopolization carries implications far beyond quarterly earnings.

Theater owners reacted with particular intensity. Michael O’Leary, who leads Cinema United representing 30,000 movie screens nationwide, called the deal an unprecedented threat and vowed aggressive opposition. His concerns center on Netflix’s track record of offering only token theatrical releases before rushing films to its platform. Markets absorbed the anxiety immediately, with shares of AMC Entertainment, IMAX and Cinemark dropping as much as 8 percent.


Labor groups mobilized with equal force. The Writers Guild of America, representing over 12,000 screenwriters, declared the merger must be blocked, framing it as exactly what antitrust laws were designed to prevent. The Teamsters’ motion picture division similarly demanded that government regulators at all levels reject the transaction.

Promises meet deep skepticism

Netflix co-chief executive Ted Sarandos attempted damage control during a Friday investor call, insisting the company would honor Warner Bros.‘ theatrical release model. He emphasized that Netflix harbors no opposition to cinema releases and promised everything currently planned for theaters would continue reaching the big screen.

Hollywood insiders greeted these assurances with profound doubt. Several agents noted the careful qualifier, right now, which leaves ample room for future policy shifts. The skepticism has roots in Sarandos’ recent statements. Just last April at the Time100 Summit, he suggested declining box office numbers showed consumers prefer watching movies at home. He went further, calling theaters an outmoded idea for most people.

Workers brace for job losses

For Los Angeles entertainment workers, from camera operators to hairstylists to set designers, the Netflix deal signals another devastating blow to an already hemorrhaging job market. The industry has shed tens of thousands of positions since 2020, battered by the pandemic, two union strikes, production fleeing to cheaper markets and artificial intelligence’s creeping automation.

Industry veterans recognize that consolidation always translates to layoffs. Disney’s $71.3 billion Fox acquisition in 2019 triggered significant job cuts. The pattern repeated when Skydance Media completed its $8 billion Paramount takeover this year, with Paramount subsequently eliminating over 2,000 workers to slash $3 billion in costs.

Representative Laura Friedman, whose Burbank district encompasses major film institutions, emphasized that repeated consolidation has already cost countless jobs. She pledged close scrutiny to ensure the deal supports Los Angeles workers rather than gutting them.

Political response stays measured

Los Angeles political leaders trod carefully in their responses. Mayor Karen Bass highlighted how production studios contribute to the city’s status as the creative capital while promising to foster local production and job creation. City Councilwoman Katy Yaroslavsky, whose district includes major union headquarters and production facilities, focused on whether the deal strengthens local production or accelerates work migration elsewhere. She acknowledged Netflix as a strong city partner but noted the entertainment sector remains in crisis.

Austin Beutner, challenging Bass in the upcoming mayoral race, expressed direct concern about consolidation’s impact on community jobs.

Behind the scenes, City Hall sources revealed mixed feelings. Some liberals quietly welcomed Netflix’s victory over David Ellison and his father Larry, a prominent Trump supporter, preventing them from acquiring another landmark studio. Yet federal regulatory approval looms ominously, particularly given what Netflix might need to do to appease the incoming administration.

Creative freedom under threat

On the entertainment-heavy Westside, the news dominated conversations. Entertainment lawyer Chris Perez, who represents independent and documentary producers, reported nonstop phone calls. When only a few buyers control the marketplace, he explained, they gravitate toward safer, less risky content. The result is diminished experimentation and reduced creative boldness. Consolidation has real potential to kill creativity, he warned.

Michele Mulroney, president of Writers Guild of America West, called the merger a disaster. She noted that Netflix‘s business model already revolves around keeping audiences glued to couches consuming content. These mergers always promise benefits, she said, but consistently deliver diminished competition, lower pay and fewer jobs for workers. She called it a very dismal day for Hollywood’s creative community.

The deal now enters complex regulatory review where political considerations, antitrust scrutiny and labor advocacy will collide. For an industry already reeling from disruption, the outcome may determine whether Hollywood’s creative workforce survives or becomes another casualty of the streaming wars.

data.title: Fortnite runs smoothly after Cloudflare outage ends
data.description:
Fortnite players experienced smooth gaming sessions on Dec. 5, with Epic Games confirming all systems operate normally following brief early morning disruptions. The popular battle royale game avoided major problems despite a wider internet infrastructure issue that temporarily affected multiple online gaming platforms.

Epic’s official status page showed no incidents for the day, with matchmaking, login systems and gameplay remaining stable across console, PC and mobile devices. The steady performance came after some players reported login delays earlier in the morning, though those issues traced back to external factors rather than Fortnite’s own servers.


Cloudflare maintenance creates temporary problems

The brief login slowdowns resulted from a Cloudflare outage tied to scheduled maintenance in Detroit. Cloudflare provides critical internet infrastructure services that many websites and online games depend on for reliable connections between players and game servers.

The maintenance window ran from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. UTC, during which some users encountered temporary errors when trying to access various online platforms. The issue triggered 5xx error responses, a technical indicator of server-side problems that prevent websites and games from functioning properly.


Fortnite never went completely offline during the Cloudflare disruption. However, several players reported slower than normal login times and matchmaking delays until Cloudflare completed its maintenance work and restored full service. Epic’s systems remained operational throughout the period, with connection difficulties stemming from the infrastructure provider rather than the game itself.

FORTNITE NEW UPDATE 39.10!

– Winterfest 2025
– Playboi Carti Skin
– Harry Potter Skin
– Harry Potter Mythic
– OG Chapter 1 Season 7 Battle Pass
– New LEGO Pass
– LEGO Ninjago
– Rocket League Pass Season 21 Car Body
– Junie Citroen 2CV Car Body
– Meow Wagon Car Body
– C3… pic.twitter.com/rSybSBIRKV

— FORTNITE STUDIOS (@FNSLEAKS) December 3, 2025

Multiple games affected by infrastructure issue

Fortnite joined other popular online games experiencing similar difficulties during the Cloudflare maintenance window. Roblox and League of Legends also faced temporary connection problems as the infrastructure outage rippled across gaming platforms that rely on Cloudflare’s services.

The widespread nature of the disruption highlighted how modern online gaming depends on interconnected internet infrastructure. When providers like Cloudflare encounter technical problems or conduct maintenance, the effects can touch numerous unrelated games and websites simultaneously.

Cloudflare has since restored all systems to operational status. No lingering effects from the maintenance window continue to affect Fortnite or other impacted platforms, with all services returning to normal performance levels.

Chapter 7 Season 1 continues without major issues

The current Fortnite season launched after the v39.00 update required several hours of planned downtime on Nov. 29. Servers reopened between 12:30 a.m. and 12:50 a.m. UTC on Nov. 30, introducing a Pacific-style map layout, updated movement mechanics and a refreshed weapon selection.

Chapter 7 Season 1 has operated smoothly since its debut, with no significant technical problems disrupting gameplay. The season runs until March 4, 2026, giving players several months to progress through the new battle pass and explore the updated content.

Fortnite Reload and Blitz Royale returned to the game on Dec. 4 following a small hotfix. That patch also added Surf City to the rotation along with themed cosmetic items, expanding the variety of gameplay experiences available to players.

According To Fortnite We’re aware players on PlayStation are currently experiencing login issues.
Our team is investigating and we’ll provide an update as soon as we have more info or a fix pic.twitter.com/PpQVdSIPqk

— Gamingforlife17 (@Kyle12262931) December 5, 2025

Next update brings winter content

Epic Games plans to release update v39.10 on Dec. 11 at 4 a.m. ET. The patch is expected to introduce Winterfest 2025, the game’s annual holiday celebration that typically begins in mid-December and features special limited-time modes, challenges and cosmetic rewards.

Maintenance windows for major Fortnite updates usually last between two and three hours. Epic announces any planned downtime through its official status page and the FortniteStatus account on X, giving players advance notice to plan their gaming sessions accordingly.

OG Mode is scheduled to return on Dec. 12, continuing the rotation of legacy content that lets players experience earlier versions of Fortnite’s map and gameplay mechanics. The mode has proven popular with longtime fans who appreciate revisiting the game’s evolution over the years.

Server stability remains strong

The last notable Fortnite service interruption occurred on Dec. 4 and was resolved in the early hours of Dec. 5. The quick resolution demonstrated Epic’s ability to address technical issues efficiently, minimizing disruption to the player experience.

Fortnite’s infrastructure has proven resilient throughout Chapter 7 Season 1, handling the player load without major crashes or extended outages. The game’s stable performance becomes particularly important during new season launches and major content updates, when player numbers typically spike as people log in to explore fresh features.

Epic maintains transparency about server status through multiple communication channels, helping players stay informed about any potential issues before they launch the game. This proactive approach reduces frustration by setting clear expectations about when systems might experience temporary problems during planned maintenance or unexpected technical difficulties.

FORTNITE CHAPTER 7 DOWNTIME SERVERS DOWN WILL TAKE SEVERAL HOURS! pic.twitter.com/4zKyvXMDqB

— Barnus (@Bxrnus) November 29, 2025

data.title: Cloudflare glitch sparks global website outage
data.description:
A widespread technical failure at Cloudflare sent ripples across the internet Friday, temporarily knocking major websites offline and raising fresh concerns about the fragility of digital infrastructure.

The U.S.-based internet infrastructure company confirmed it had deployed a fix for problems affecting its dashboard and related applications. The announcement came after users worldwide reported being unable to access dozens of popular platforms and services that rely on Cloudflare’s network.


Market response and immediate impact

Investors reacted swiftly to news of the disruption. Cloudflare shares dropped as much as 4.5 percent in premarket trading as the scope of the outage became apparent. The stock recovered somewhat after the company announced it had implemented a solution and was monitoring results, but remained down roughly 2 percent.

The outage affected an impressive roster of high-traffic sites. LinkedIn, the professional networking platform, experienced accessibility problems during the incident. Coinbase, a prominent digital currency exchange, also struggled to maintain service. Substack, the online publishing platform that has become increasingly popular among independent writers and journalists, was among those impacted.


Widespread disruptions across industries

Downdetector, a service that tracks website outages, recorded a sharp surge in user reports around 9:16 a.m. London time. The monitoring site itself briefly experienced difficulties, highlighting how interconnected modern web infrastructure has become.

Users reported problems accessing e-commerce platform Shopify, banking institution HSBC, and food delivery service Deliveroo, among numerous others. These complaints subsided relatively quickly once Cloudflare engineers rolled out their fix.

The incident underscored how dependent the modern internet has become on a small number of infrastructure providers. When one experiences technical difficulties, the effects can cascade across countless websites and services simultaneously.

A concerning pattern

This marks the second significant Cloudflare disruption in less than three weeks. The previous crash generated error messages across the internet, prompting company representatives to describe the situation as unacceptable given how essential their services have become to daily web operations.

The repeat occurrence raises questions about system redundancy and the concentration of internet infrastructure among relatively few companies. For businesses that depend on continuous online availability, even brief outages can result in lost revenue and diminished customer confidence.

The critical infrastructure provider

Cloudflare has established itself as a cornerstone of internet operations, managing and securing traffic for approximately 20 percent of the web. The company provides multiple essential services that help keep websites running smoothly and securely.

Among its most important offerings is protection against distributed denial of service attacks. These cyber assaults occur when malicious actors flood a website with so many traffic requests that the system becomes overwhelmed and stops functioning properly. Without robust defenses, many businesses would find themselves vulnerable to such disruptions.

Beyond security, Cloudflare optimizes how content moves across the internet. Its network acts as an intermediary between websites and their visitors, speeding up load times and improving overall user experience. For companies operating globally, these performance enhancements prove invaluable.

Broader implications for digital reliability

The outage amplifies ongoing debates about internet infrastructure resilience. While consolidation offers benefits like specialized expertise and economies of scale, it also creates single points of failure that can affect vast portions of the web simultaneously.

As more commerce, communication and essential services migrate online, the stakes surrounding infrastructure reliability continue rising. Friday’s disruption, though relatively brief, demonstrated how quickly normal digital operations can grind to a halt when foundational systems falter.

The incident serves as a reminder that the internet’s apparent stability masks underlying vulnerabilities. Complex technical systems, no matter how sophisticated, remain susceptible to failures that can ripple across the digital landscape.

For Cloudflare, the challenge now involves preventing future occurrences and rebuilding confidence among the countless businesses and platforms that depend on its services. The company’s response to this latest crisis, and its ability to implement more robust safeguards, will likely influence how the industry approaches infrastructure reliability going forward.

data.title: Meta surges 6% on massive metaverse spending cuts
data.description:
Investors sent Meta Platforms stock soaring more than 6% Thursday morning after reports emerged that CEO Mark Zuckerberg is preparing to dramatically scale back the company’s controversial metaverse investments. The surprising reversal marks a significant shift in strategy for the tech giant that has poured billions into virtual reality technology over the past several years.

The proposed cuts could reach 30%

According to Bloomberg News, Meta executives have engaged in serious discussions about reducing the metaverse division’s budget by as much as 30% heading into 2026. This substantial reduction would impact the Meta Horizon Worlds platform and the Quest virtual reality hardware unit, two cornerstone projects of the company’s metaverse vision. Budget cuts of this magnitude would almost certainly trigger workforce reductions, with potential layoffs beginning as early as January. However, company insiders stress that leadership has not finalized any decisions regarding the exact scale or timing of these changes.


Hawaii meetings shaped the new direction

The proposed reductions emerged from Meta’s annual budget planning process, which included a series of strategic meetings held at Zuckerberg‘s Hawaii compound last month. During these sessions, the CEO reportedly asked executives across the company to identify potential cuts of around 10% in their respective departments, a standard request that has appeared in similar budget cycles during previous years. The metaverse group, however, faces significantly deeper reductions than other divisions, suggesting a fundamental reassessment of priorities rather than routine cost management.

Competition never materialized as expected

Sources familiar with the internal discussions revealed that Meta has not encountered the level of industry-wide competition in metaverse technology that company leadership once anticipated when making initial investment decisions. This lack of competitive pressure has apparently influenced the decision to pull back resources from what was once positioned as the company’s defining bet on the future of social interaction and digital experiences. The virtual reality group, which accounts for the largest share of metaverse-related spending, would absorb the majority of proposed cuts, along with significant reductions to Horizon Worlds development.


Investor frustration drove the reassessment

The metaverse investments have faced mounting criticism from Meta’s investor base, who increasingly view the spending as a drain on resources that could be better allocated elsewhere. The stock’s enthusiastic response to news of potential cuts demonstrates how deeply concerned shareholders had become about the company’s commitment to a technology that has yet to achieve mainstream adoption or generate meaningful revenue. Wall Street analysts have repeatedly questioned whether the massive investments would ever deliver returns justifying their cost, creating pressure on leadership to reconsider the strategy.

What this means for Meta’s future focus

The dramatic shift away from metaverse spending signals that Meta may be pivoting resources toward other priorities, potentially including artificial intelligence development, traditional social media platforms or new revenue-generating initiatives. The company has continued investing heavily in AI technology even while pursuing metaverse projects, and scaling back virtual reality spending could free up significant capital and talent for these efforts. Zuckerberg’s willingness to dramatically reduce a project he personally championed suggests pragmatic recognition that the metaverse vision may need far more time to mature than originally projected.

The timing raises strategic questions

The proposed cuts come at an interesting moment for Meta, as the broader technology industry grapples with economic uncertainty and increased scrutiny of spending efficiency. Many tech companies have implemented workforce reductions and budget trimming over the past two years, prioritizing profitability over aggressive expansion into unproven markets. Meta’s metaverse pullback fits this pattern while also acknowledging that consumer interest in virtual reality experiences has not accelerated as quickly as company projections suggested it would.

Reality check for virtual ambitions

For employees working on metaverse projects, the news creates uncertainty about job security and the future direction of their work. The potential January timeline for layoffs means affected workers could face employment changes early in the new year. Meanwhile, the broader tech industry will be watching closely to see whether other companies pursuing similar virtual reality initiatives follow Meta’s lead in reassessing their commitments to this technology sector that has struggled to move beyond niche gaming and entertainment applications into the mainstream digital lifestyle product that advocates envisioned.

Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and not financial advice. Always research before making investment decisions.

This article is based on reporting from Investing.com and Bloomberg News.

data.title: Salesforce’s impressive Q3 results spark stock surge
data.description:
Salesforce delivered earnings results on Wednesday that sent its stock soaring in after-hours trading, as the enterprise software giant reported stronger-than-expected performance across its business. The company’s third-quarter fiscal 2026 results showcased substantial profit growth and momentum from its newest artificial intelligence products.

The San Francisco-based tech company posted revenue of $10.3 billion for the three-month period, representing approximately 9.5% growth compared to the same quarter last year. That top-line expansion came alongside even more impressive bottom-line improvements, with net income surging 40% year over year to reach $2.1 billion. Diluted earnings per share climbed roughly 39% to $2.19, exceeding analyst expectations.


Stock market responds enthusiastically

Investors reacted swiftly to the positive results, pushing Salesforce shares up around 8% in after-hours trading following the report’s release. The stock movement reflected market confidence in the company’s strategic direction and its ability to capitalize on enterprise demand for artificial intelligence capabilities integrated into business software platforms.

The enthusiasm stems partly from management’s decision to raise full-year revenue guidance, now projecting fiscal 2026 revenue between $41.45 billion and $41.55 billion. This upward revision signals confidence that strong performance will continue through the remainder of the fiscal year.


AI products drive momentum

Salesforce’s newest artificial intelligence offerings are generating substantial traction in the market. The company’s Agentforce and Data 360 products have reached nearly $1.4 billion in annual recurring revenue, marking an explosive 114% increase compared to the prior year. This dramatic growth rate demonstrates that enterprises are eager to adopt AI-powered tools that promise to transform how businesses operate and engage with customers.

The adoption metrics tell an equally compelling story. Salesforce now counts over 9,500 paid Agentforce deals, indicating widespread customer interest beyond just pilot programs or trials. The platform has processed 3.2 trillion tokens, a measure of the computational work performed by AI systems. This massive volume underscores how deeply customers are integrating these tools into their daily operations rather than treating them as experimental add-ons.

Understanding the revenue pipeline

Current remaining performance obligations, abbreviated as cRPO, came in at $29.4 billion for the third quarter, growing 11% year over year. This metric represents contracted revenue that has not yet been recognized, essentially providing visibility into future revenue streams. The strong cRPO growth signals healthy demand and suggests the company has built a robust pipeline of business that will convert to revenue in coming quarters.

For companies like Salesforce that operate on subscription models, cRPO serves as an important indicator of business momentum beyond just quarterly revenue figures. The 11% growth rate indicates customers are signing contracts for Salesforce services at an accelerating pace.

The agentic enterprise vision

CEO Marc Benioff emphasized the company’s focus on building what he termed the agentic enterprise, a concept centered on AI agents that can autonomously perform tasks traditionally requiring human intervention. This vision represents Salesforce’s bet that the next phase of enterprise software will involve intelligent systems that don’t just assist workers but actively handle complex workflows independently.

The strong financial results suggest this strategic direction is resonating with customers who see practical applications for AI agents in sales, customer service, marketing and other business functions. Rather than simply adding AI features for competitive positioning, Salesforce appears to be driving real adoption that translates into measurable business outcomes for clients.

The combination of solid core business performance, explosive AI product growth and raised guidance paints a picture of a company successfully navigating the transition to an AI-powered future while maintaining its dominant position in customer relationship management software.

Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only and not financial advice. Always research before making investment decisions.

Source: Sarajevo Times/Christian Baha

data.title: Meta’s WhatsApp AI sparks major European investigation
data.description:
The European Commission has launched a new antitrust investigation into Meta Platforms, focusing on how the tech giant rolled out artificial intelligence features within WhatsApp. The move represents the latest effort by European regulators to scrutinize Big Tech’s expanding use of generative AI technology.

Brussels announced the investigation Thursday, confirming earlier reports about the probe that examines Meta’s policies surrounding AI provider access to the popular messaging service. The inquiry comes after Meta integrated its proprietary Meta AI system into WhatsApp earlier this year, raising questions about fair competition in the rapidly evolving AI marketplace.


What triggered the investigation

The European Commission opened its investigation specifically targeting Meta’s new policy regarding how AI providers can access WhatsApp. The scrutiny intensified after the California-based company built its Meta AI chatbot and virtual assistant directly into WhatsApp’s interface starting in March 2025 across European markets.

European regulators are examining whether Meta leveraged its dominant position in messaging services to give preferential treatment to its own AI products while potentially blocking or limiting access for competing AI chatbot providers. The investigation reflects growing concerns about how major technology companies might use their control over popular platforms to extend their influence into emerging AI markets.


Meta has pushed back firmly against the allegations, with a WhatsApp spokesperson calling the claims baseless. The company argues that adding chatbots to its platform creates significant technical challenges that its systems were not originally designed to handle.

Meta’s defense of its AI integration

The tech giant maintains that the AI marketplace remains highly competitive, with users having numerous ways to access the services they prefer. According to Meta, consumers can reach various AI tools through app stores, search engines, email services, partnership integrations and operating systems, suggesting its WhatsApp integration doesn’t limit consumer choice.

The company’s position emphasizes the strain that chatbot integration places on existing infrastructure, presenting the limitations as technical necessities rather than competitive barriers. However, regulators appear unconvinced that technical challenges alone justify Meta’s approach to AI provider access on its platform.

The investigation puts Meta in an increasingly difficult position as it attempts to expand its AI offerings while operating under intense regulatory scrutiny in Europe. The company has invested heavily in developing and promoting Meta AI across its family of apps, including Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp, as it competes with rivals like OpenAI, Google and Anthropic in the generative AI space.

Italy takes parallel action

The European Commission’s investigation isn’t the only regulatory challenge Meta faces regarding its WhatsApp AI integration. Italy’s antitrust watchdog opened its own parallel investigation in July, examining allegations that Meta abused its market power by integrating an AI tool into the messaging platform.

Italian regulators expanded their probe in November to specifically examine whether Meta further abused its dominant position by blocking rival AI chatbots from accessing WhatsApp. The dual investigations from both EU-level and national regulators demonstrate the breadth of concern about Meta’s AI strategy among European authorities.

The coordinated regulatory attention suggests that competition officials across Europe view Meta’s approach to AI integration as potentially problematic for maintaining fair competition in the technology sector. Both investigations could result in significant fines or mandatory changes to how Meta operates its AI services within the European market.

Legal framework and broader implications

Officials indicated that the EU probe will proceed under traditional antitrust rules rather than the bloc’s Digital Markets Act, the landmark legislation currently being used to examine other Big Tech practices. The commission is separately using the Digital Markets Act to scrutinize Amazon and Microsoft’s cloud services for potential anticompetitive behavior.

The choice to pursue the investigation under conventional antitrust law rather than newer digital market regulations suggests regulators believe Meta’s alleged conduct falls under established competition principles. Traditional antitrust cases can take years to resolve but carry substantial penalties if violations are found.

The investigation represents part of a broader European effort to balance support for technological innovation with measures to prevent large companies from using their market dominance to stifle competition. European regulators have consistently taken a more aggressive stance toward Big Tech regulation than their American counterparts, leading to numerous high-profile investigations and penalties against major technology firms.

What happens next for Meta

As the investigation proceeds, Meta will need to provide detailed information about its AI integration policies and technical decisions to European regulators. The company may face requests for internal communications and documentation explaining how decisions were made regarding third-party AI provider access to WhatsApp.

The outcome could significantly impact how Meta and other major technology companies approach AI integration across their platforms in Europe. If regulators determine that Meta violated competition rules, potential remedies could include substantial fines, mandatory access provisions for competing AI services, or structural changes to how Meta AI operates within WhatsApp.

The case highlights the growing tension between Big Tech’s ambitions in artificial intelligence and regulatory efforts to ensure competitive markets remain open to new entrants and existing rivals. As AI technology becomes increasingly central to consumer services, expect similar regulatory scrutiny of other major platform operators integrating proprietary AI systems.

data.title: Jensen Huang explains why enterprise AI is everything
data.description:
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sat down with CNBC on December 1 to discuss a partnership that he believes will revolutionize how the world approaches design and engineering. The conversation revealed a strategic vision that extends far beyond the chatbots and consumer applications currently dominating headlines about artificial intelligence.

Huang joined Synopsys CEO Sassine Ghazi to announce an expanded collaboration that transforms one of the most compute-intensive industries in existence. Synopsys is pivoting its entire company to build GPU-accelerated tools on Nvidia‘s CUDA platform, physical AI and Omniverse technologies. This shift affects everything from electronic design automation to system design automation to computer-aided engineering and drug discovery.


The decade-long preparation

The partnership represents the culmination of work that began years ago when Huang first started building the software stack necessary for this transformation. For over three decades, the design and engineering industry has relied on tools that ran exclusively on CPUs. Nvidia’s technology now enables simulations at speeds and scales previously unimaginable, allowing companies to complete entire engineering projects inside digital twins before building physical prototypes.

Huang emphasized that while consumer AI applications like ChatGPT capture public attention, the real opportunity lies in enterprise and industrial sectors. He explained that getting answers 90% correct works fine for recommending movies or advertisements, but industries designing cars, planes and factories require perfection because so much hangs in the balance.


The platform shift nobody sees

A striking statistic illustrates the transformation already underway. In 2016, the world’s scientific supercomputers were 90% CPU-based and 10% GPU-based. This year, those numbers have completely flipped, with 90% running on GPUs and only 10% on CPUs. This platform shift happened in scientific computing over the past decade, and Huang believes the engineering industry is now reaching that same tipping point.

The Synopsys partnership expands significantly with the company’s recent acquisition of Ansys, which brings sophisticated engineering solutions for automotive, robotics, drones and industrial machinery into the fold. Ghazi noted that designing these increasingly sophisticated, AI-driven systems requires advanced engineering solutions to manage complexity while keeping costs affordable and timelines reasonable.

Understanding the market size

Huang provided context that helps explain why he considers industrial AI more significant than consumer applications. Industrial companies typically spend hundreds of millions or low billions of dollars on engineering software tools. However, those same companies spend 10 to 20 times more on physically prototyping products. Nvidia itself spends hundreds of millions on design tools but billions on prototyping.

The opportunity emerges when companies can prototype everything digitally through simulations and digital twins, eliminating waste from physical prototyping. This shift increases the market opportunity by a factor of 10 to 100, affecting an industrial sector measured in trillions of dollars rather than billions.

Why this matters now

When asked about concerns over AI spending by hyperscalers and data center investments, Huang reframed the conversation. He explained that the world’s major cloud providers would have made this platform shift from general-purpose computing to accelerated computing regardless of chatbots or agentic AI. Moore’s law has slowed tremendously, and the industry needs more powerful, efficient computing methods going forward.

The technology reaching industrial applications represents a critical milestone because these sectors cannot adopt solutions that work 90% of the time. They require extraordinary precision and reliability, which takes longer to develop but serves vastly larger markets once achieved.

Ghazi added that workloads previously requiring two to three weeks can now be reduced to hours through GPU acceleration, delivering transformative value to customers. Companies across automotive, aerospace and industrial sectors will need to increase their R&D spending on automation and software exponentially to remain competitive as products grow more sophisticated.

Huang also addressed competition from custom chips, noting that Nvidia’s CUDA platform offers versatility and fungibility that application-specific integrated circuits cannot match. The Synopsys partnership demonstrates opportunities only available through Nvidia’s broader platform, which operates across every cloud provider, original equipment manufacturer, on-premise installation and edge deployment.

Source: CNBC

data.title: Can’t find your Spotify Wrapped 2025? Try this fix
data.description:
Spotify Wrapped 2025 has arrived, giving users a chance to see their top songs, artists, albums, and podcasts from the year. Many users are noticing that their personalized recaps are not appearing, creating frustration for those eager to review their listening habits. The main reason Wrapped might not show up is that the Spotify app is outdated or experiencing heavy traffic from millions of users accessing the service at the same time.

For iOS users, running app version 9.1.0 is recommended, while Android users should ensure their version is 9.1.0.1147. Updating the app often resolves most display problems and allows the Wrapped banner to appear properly. Regularly updating Spotify also ensures access to the newest features and smoother playback overall.


Accessing Spotify Wrapped 2025 manually

If updating the app does not bring up the Wrapped banner, users can still find their recap through a few simple steps. Opening the Spotify app and typing “2025 Wrapped” in the search bar should bring up the playlist. Searching for “Wrapped” along with the year can also help locate previous years’ recaps.

Desktop users can visit spotify.com/wrapped and click the Download Spotify button to go directly to the 2025 recap. This method works even when the app fails to display the personalized banner. Users who primarily listen on desktop devices often find this approach the fastest way to view their top songs and podcasts for the year.


Troubleshooting when Wrapped doesn’t load

Some users continue to experience issues even after updating the app. Uninstalling and reinstalling Spotify often resolves lingering problems. Another method involves entering “spotify:datastories:wrapped” into the app’s search bar to manually open the Wrapped 2025 slideshow. These solutions usually allow users to access their top songs, podcasts, and artists for the year without further delay.

Spotify Wrapped can also be affected by network issues or server congestion. Connecting to a stable Wi-Fi network and restarting the device can sometimes resolve loading problems. Additionally, clearing the app cache on Android or reinstalling the app on iOS can improve performance and make the Wrapped banner appear.

Spotify listening age and Apple users

Spotify Wrapped also provides insights into a user’s listening age, showing which music eras are most represented in their playlists. This feature has become a popular way to compare listening habits with friends and across platforms, including Apple devices. Many users enjoy checking top genres, favorite podcasts, and Wrapped 2025 artists to see how their music taste stacks up against broader trends.

Even after using all these steps, some users may need to wait a few hours for the Wrapped banner to appear. Manually searching for “2025 Wrapped” or reinstalling the app remains the most reliable solution to access the recap. Spotify Wrapped continues to be a yearly highlight, offering fans a way to reflect on their listening habits while discovering trends in the global music community.

FAQs about Spotify wrapped 2025

Why is my Spotify Wrapped not showing up?

The app may be outdated or facing temporary server delays. Updating to the latest version typically resolves this issue.

Where can I find Spotify Wrapped 2025?

Users can type “2025 Wrapped” into the Spotify search bar, check the home tab, or visit spotify.com/wrapped in a browser.

Why isn’t Spotify Wrapped appearing on Apple devices?

iOS users may face delays in the rollout. Updating the app and logging out and back in can help ensure the Wrapped stats appear.

What is Spotify Listening Age?

Listening age reflects the era of music most represented in your playlists, while Wrapped 2025 highlights top artists, genres, and overall listening trends for the year.

Spotify Wrapped continues to be a yearly highlight for millions of users, providing a unique way to review musical habits. Following these steps ensures that every listener can access their personalized 2025 recap without missing out.

data.title: Microsoft’s stunning Azure growth hits 39% milestone
data.description:
Microsoft continues to dominate the cloud computing landscape with impressive numbers that showcase the company’s position at the forefront of the artificial intelligence revolution. The tech giant’s Azure platform posted remarkable revenue growth that exceeded expectations and demonstrated the insatiable appetite for AI capabilities across industries.

According to Baron Fifth Avenue Growth Fund’s third quarter investor letter, Azure revenue accelerated to 39% growth during the period. The investment management firm highlighted Microsoft’s performance as evidence of the massive opportunity unfolding in the AI sector, both in terms of productivity improvements and revenue generation for companies embracing the technology.


Meeting unprecedented demand

The explosive growth comes despite Microsoft struggling to keep pace with customer demand. The Redmond-based company has been working overtime to expand its infrastructure, bringing an astounding 2 gigawatts of new data center capacity online just in the past 12 months. For perspective, that’s enough power to supply a small city, and it’s all dedicated to processing the compute-intensive workloads that modern AI applications require.

Even with this massive expansion, demand continues to outstrip supply. Businesses across every sector are racing to implement AI solutions, from customer service chatbots to complex data analysis tools, and they need cloud infrastructure to power these applications. Microsoft’s Azure platform has positioned itself as the go-to solution for many of these enterprises.


The AI explosion in numbers

Perhaps the most eye-opening statistic from Microsoft‘s recent performance involves its AI foundry usage. The platform processed over 500 trillion tokens during the quarter, representing a staggering 700% increase compared to the same period last year. For those unfamiliar with the term, tokens are the basic units of text that AI models process, and this astronomical number reflects how rapidly AI adoption has accelerated across Microsoft’s customer base.

This exponential growth in AI usage translates directly to revenue, as companies pay for the computing power needed to run these sophisticated models. The more tokens processed, the more Microsoft earns from its cloud services.

Investor confidence remains strong

Microsoft’s performance hasn’t gone unnoticed by Wall Street’s biggest players. At the end of the third quarter, 312 hedge funds held positions in Microsoft stock, up from 294 in the previous quarter. This increase in institutional ownership signals continued confidence in the company’s growth trajectory despite the stock’s already massive $3.642 trillion market capitalization.

The company closed at $490 per share on December 2, with shares gaining 12.02% over the past 52 weeks. While the stock experienced a minor pullback with a one-month return of negative 3.38%, the longer-term trend remains solidly positive.

Broader financial picture

Microsoft reported revenue of $77.7 billion in the first quarter of fiscal year 2026, representing 18% growth year-over-year. When adjusted for constant currency to account for exchange rate fluctuations, the growth rate came in at 17%. These numbers demonstrate that Azure’s success is part of a broader story of strong performance across Microsoft’s diverse portfolio of products and services.

Baron Fifth Avenue Growth Fund, which featured Microsoft prominently in its quarterly letter, posted a 5.7% gain during the third quarter. While this trailed the Russell 1000 Growth Index’s 10.5% return and the S&P 500’s 8.1% gain, the fund has delivered solid returns of 14.4% year-to-date.

Looking ahead

The continued acceleration of Azure‘s growth rate suggests Microsoft has found itself in the enviable position of riding a massive technology wave that shows no signs of cresting. As more companies discover practical applications for AI and invest in cloud infrastructure to support these initiatives, Microsoft stands to benefit as one of the primary providers of the computing power that makes it all possible.

Source: Insider Monkey/Baron Funds

data.title: YouTube challenges Spotify with new recap feature
data.description:
The music streaming wars have expanded beyond playlists and podcasts. YouTube is making its boldest move yet into year-end recap territory, launching YouTube Recap on Dec. 2 as a direct challenger to Spotify Wrapped and Apple Music Replay. The Google-owned platform’s new feature promises to transform how users engage with their annual viewing habits, offering a personalized highlight reel that captures 12 months of digital consumption.

The timing couldn’t be more strategic. As Spotify Wrapped continues to dominate social media feeds each December, YouTube recognized an opportunity to claim its share of the viral year-end conversation. The platform isn’t simply mimicking competitors—it’s leveraging its unique position as the world’s largest video-sharing service to create something distinctly its own.


Understanding YouTube’s latest innovation

YouTube Recap represents the platform’s answer to a cultural phenomenon that has become as predictable as holiday traditions. The feature synthesizes users’ watch history throughout 2025 into a shareable digital experience. Unlike audio-focused competitors, YouTube’s version captures the full spectrum of content consumption: music videos, tutorials, gaming streams, podcasts and everything in between.

The recap functionality delivers customized insights into viewing patterns, revealing users’ top channels, dominant interests and even an algorithmic “personality type” based on consumption habits. This psychological element adds a layer of self-discovery that transforms raw data into narrative storytelling. Users receive not just statistics, but a reflection of their digital identity shaped over 365 days of clicks, views and rabbit holes.


The platform’s approach acknowledges a fundamental truth about modern media consumption: video has become the universal language of the internet. By creating a recap that encompasses this diversity, YouTube positions itself as the definitive record of how people spent their screen time.

Accessing your digital year in review

Finding YouTube Recap requires minimal effort, designed for immediate gratification. The feature launches Tuesday, appearing prominently on the YouTube homepage for desktop users. Mobile users can locate their personalized recap under the “You” tab, ensuring accessibility across devices. This streamlined distribution reflects lessons learned from competitors—make sharing effortless, and users become unpaid marketing ambassadors.

The rollout strategy capitalizes on peak social media activity during early December, when users actively seek shareable content that sparks conversations and comparisons. YouTube understands that these recaps function as social currency, prompting discussions about taste, habits and the surprising discoveries that algorithms surface.

Cultural moments that defined the platform

Alongside the personalized recaps, YouTube released five comprehensive year-end lists that capture the platform’s cultural pulse in 2025. These rankings offer fascinating insights into collective viewing behavior, revealing trends that transcended individual bubbles to achieve mainstream recognition.

The trending topics list showcases remarkable diversity. Squid Game maintains its grip on popular imagination, while user-generated Roblox experiences demonstrate gaming’s creative evolution. KPop Demon Hunters emerged as a genre-defying phenomenon, blending Korean pop aesthetics with supernatural storytelling. The inclusion of “brainrot”—internet slang for addictive, often absurd content—acknowledges the platform’s role in shaping digital vernacular.

Political commentator Charlie Kirk’s appearance signals YouTube’s growing importance as a political media platform. Meanwhile, Labubu, the Nintendo Switch 2 and Cookie Run Kingdom highlight how YouTube serves diverse communities from collectors to gamers. Blue Lock represents anime’s continued dominance, while KATSEYE demonstrates K-pop’s expanding global footprint.

Creators who captured millions

The top creators list, based on subscriber growth within the United States, reveals the platform’s creator economy hierarchy. MrBeast continues his unprecedented reign, followed by gaming-focused CaylusBlox and entertainment powerhouse IShowSpeed. These names represent different facets of YouTube success: elaborate stunts, gaming commentary and unfiltered personality-driven content.

Emerging voices like Double Date, Cadel and Mia, and Law by Mike demonstrate audience appetite for relationship content and accessible legal education. The return of CoryxKenshin after extended breaks proves that authentic creator-audience relationships transcend posting schedules. Zack D. Films and Outdoor Boys represent the enduring appeal of educational and nature content, offering counterpoints to the platform’s entertainment-heavy reputation.

Podcasts command attention

YouTube’s podcast rankings, measured by total watch time, confirm the platform’s emergence as a serious audio-visual competitor to traditional podcast apps. The Joe Rogan Experience claims the top position, validating YouTube’s appeal for long-form conversation. KILL TONY and Good Mythical Morning demonstrate comedy’s stronghold, while Rotten Mango proves true crime’s unshakeable audience devotion.

Political commentary appears through The MeidasTouch Podcast, while legacy media maintains relevance with 48 Hours. Shawn Ryan Show captures military and intelligence community interest, Smosh Reads Reddit Stories mines internet culture, and This Past Weekend w/ Theo Von alongside The Diary Of A CEO offer distinct takes on interview-based programming.

Music that moved the algorithm

The platform’s music rankings split into two categories: songs dominating Shorts creation and those generating massive view counts. On Shorts, Forrest Frank’s “YOUR WAY’S BETTER” led creators’ soundtrack choices, followed by ATLXS’s slowed remix and Black Eyed Peas’ enduring “Rock That Body.” Connie Francis enjoyed unexpected revival with “Pretty Little Baby,” while Joyful’s “chess” and Doechii’s “Anxiety” represented contemporary sounds.

The KPop Demon Hunters soundtrack proved formidable, placing multiple tracks across categories. Jiandro’s “Confess your love” and Billie Eilish’s Ocean Eyes demonstrated how songs achieve longevity through creator adoption, often years after initial release.

For overall views, Bruno Mars emerged as the year’s dominant collaborator, appearing on both “Die With A Smile” with Lady Gaga and ROSÉ’s “APT.” The KPop Demon Hunters phenomenon placed four tracks in the top ten, confirming the show’s cultural impact. Kendrick Lamar’s presence with “Luther” featuring SZA and “tv off” reinforced hip-hop’s consistent YouTube appeal.

YouTube Recap arrives not as a copycat feature but as the platform’s declaration that video consumption deserves equal celebration alongside audio streaming. As users begin sharing their personalized summaries, the feature’s success will depend on whether it captures imagination the way Spotify Wrapped has for years. The answer will shape how platforms compete for year-end attention in the increasingly crowded attention economy.
